heading: Built-in Methods
body:
The Node.js driver provides the following methods to perform compound
operations:

- [findOneAndDelete()](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/classes/Collection.html#findOneAndDelete)

- [findOneAndUpdate()](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/classes/Collection.html#findOneAndUpdate)

- [findOneAndReplace()](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/classes/Collection.html#findOneAndReplace)

These methods accept an optional `options` object with
configurable [sort()](node-fundamentals-sort) and
[Specify Which Fields to Return](node-fundamentals-project) options.

You can also set the `includeResultMetadata`
option to specify the return type of each
of these methods. To learn more about this option, see the
[includeResultMetadata Option](node-compound-metadata-option)
section of this guide.

The `findOneAndUpdate()` and `findOneAndDelete()` methods take the
`returnDocument` setting, which specifies if the method returns the
pre-update or post-update version of the modified document.
